description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a classic double-wing structure, featuring a radial engine and a two-bladed propeller at the nose. The fuselage is painted primarily white with blue, yellow, and red roundels and stripes, typical of Royal Air Force markings. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a small windshield for the pilot. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tailwheel, all fixed and non-retractable. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, with visible control surfaces. The wings are braced with struts and wires, giving the aircraft a robust, early 20th-century appearance.
description: A vintage white biplane with RAF markings, open cockpit, double wings, and fixed landing gear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the radial engine, propeller, and the stacked biplane wings with roundels.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the RAF insignia, open cockpit, and fixed landing gear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, horizontal and vertical stabilizers, and the double wings.
description_view_3: Right side view with visible tail number, cockpit, and wing struts.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by struts and wires; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-bladed propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two fixed main wheels and a small fixed tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located just behind the upper wing, single-seat with a small windshield.
